hero.title: Caribbean Guard
hero.tagline: Building a stronger, safer community in the water.
hero.summary: A community-led aquatic-safety organization serving Costa Rica's South Caribbean through lifesaving training, swimming education, patrols, prevention, and emergency-response coordination.

emergency.disclaimer: Whappin does not monitor emergencies or dispatch rescuers. In an emergency, always call 911 first.
emergency.networkSummary: Caribbean Guard operates a community emergency-alert network of 70+ trained members including lifeguards, surfers, swimmers, fishers, divers, and freedivers. When an emergency is reported, a protocol is activated and nearby qualified community members may respond.
emergency.demoInstructions: This is a demonstration of a future integration. In production, this would activate Caribbean Guard's emergency-alert network. No alert is actually sent.

impact.label: People trained or involved
impact.attribution: Reported by Caribbean Guard as of September 2024.

programs.title: Lifesaving Club
programs.summary: Continuing education, lifeguard courses, CPR and first-aid instructor training, swimming-instructor training, junior lifeguard / Nipper training, and ELLIS CPR training.
programs.audience: Open to community members interested in water safety
programs.schedule: Saturday morning training (location varies with ocean conditions)
programs.patrolInfo: Patrols Playa Grande and Playa Chiquita on Sundays, with additional patrols during Easter, holidays, and special dates

programs.title: Swim Club
programs.summary: Free community swim sessions open to all.
programs.schedule: Punta Uva: Thursdays 7:15 AM | Playa Negra: Tuesdays & Fridays 4:00 PM

programs.title: Swim School
programs.summary: Free swimming instruction for local children.

programs.title: Programa Playa Organizada
programs.summary: Organized Beach Program including red-and-yellow-flag supervised bathing zones, survival lines, drone mapping, rescue stations, emergency plans, rapid-response groups, and CPR/lifeguard training for local workers.

programs.title: Emergency-Alert Network
programs.summary: A community emergency group of 70+ trained members who may respond when a protocol is activated.

team.name: Lucas Iturriza
team.bio: Co-founded Caribbean Guard in March 2021, organizing community patrols during the Easter swell.

team.name: Joel Gagg
team.bio: Collaborated with Lucas Iturriza to organize the first community patrols and expand the initiative.
